dialogue: SUBJECT: ASTRID ST JAMES: "IN 1963 YALE UNIVERSITY PSYCHOLOGIST STANLEY MILGRAM RAN AN EXPERIMENT WHERE ONE GROUP OF TEST SUBJECTS ('TEACHERS') WOULD QUIZ OTHER TEST SUBJECTS ('LEARNERS') ABOUT GENERAL KNOWLEDGE."

IF THE LEARNER GOT AN ANSWER WRONG, THE TEACHER WOULD BE INSTRUCTED TO ZAP THEM WITH EVER STRONGER ELECTRIC SHOCKS. THE RESULTS PROVED THAT ORDINARY PEOPLE - IF INSTRUCTED TO DO SO BY A PERSON IN A PRESUMED POSITION OF AUTHORITY - WOULD TORTURE COMPLETE STRANGERS.

IN THE ORIGINAL MILGRAM EXPERIMENT THE LEARNERS WERE PAID ACTORS. IN THIS CONTEMPORARY RETURN OF THE EXPERIMENT, THE TESTERS COULD TORTURE THE LEARNERS IN A VR WORLD WHERE THERE WERE NO LIMITS ON THE SEVERITY OF THE PAIN THEY COULD INFLICT...
